title: Phase 4e: WebAssembly pure-browser client
description:
Compile nie-core to WebAssembly so a user can chat (and eventually pay) directly in a browser tab with no local daemon or installed software. Key material lives in browser storage — weaker than a native process but good for onboarding and low-trust devices.

## Scope

### Phase 1: chat-only WASM build
- Compile nie-core to wasm32-unknown-unknown with wasm-pack
- Audit all deps for WASM compatibility:
  - getrandom: requires js feature (getrandom/js)
  - ring/aws-lc-rs: does not support WASM — must replace with pure-Rust alternatives (ed25519-dalek already used, should be fine)
  - tokio: replace with wasm-bindgen-futures + browser setTimeout for async
  - SQLite (sqlx): not available in WASM — replace wallet store with IndexedDB (idb crate or raw web-sys bindings)
- Identity key storage: SubtleCrypto non-extractable key in IndexedDB (best available in browser)
- WebSocket: use browser native WebSocket (web-sys), not tokio-tungstenite
- UI: reuse nie-web JS/HTML from nie-0tvc, replace daemon API calls with direct WASM function calls
- Implements JSON-RPC 2.0 client (nie-hlns) directly in the browser

### Phase 2: wallet in WASM (deferred, hard)
- Sapling proving parameters (~47 MB download) must be fetched and cached in browser (Cache API)
- Sapling circuit (bellman/groth16) in WASM: possible but slow (~10-30s prove time without SIMD)
- WASM SIMD (wasm32 simd128) can reduce prove time; requires opt-in and browser support check
- Alternative: offload proving to a trusted server (breaks privacy model) — document the tradeoff explicitly

## What does NOT work in WASM
- Persistent background connection (browser kills tabs)
- Filesystem access for key backup (offer export/import flow instead)
- Native push notifications without a service worker

## Dependencies
- nie-hlns (JSON-RPC protocol) — WASM client implements the wire protocol directly
- nie-0tvc (JSON daemon + browser client) — shares the nie-web HTML/JS UI layer
notes: Review converged after 2 rounds. Round 1: P0=1, P1=7, P2=9 — all fixed. Round 2: P0=0 P1=0 P2=0. Review epics: nie-7bv6 (round 1), nie-rwr0 (round 2). 7 P3 concerns tracked as backlog.
